body:
- [x] Read UQ parameters from TOML file. UQ parameters come with prior distributions and constraints (see `ParameterDistributionStorage`), which have to be reconstructed from the parsed TOML file
- [x] Write UQ parameters to file, 
- [x] Implement "broadcasting solution" for defining priors of array-valued parameters (use case: a 100-dim parameter of weights of a neural network, each of which has the same prior distribution. Users should not have to write down 100 parameter distributions in the parameter toml file for cases like this one - rather, we want them to be able to write down a single distribution that gets broadcast and applied to each of the 100 weights).
- [x] Clean up: consistent formatting, consistent naming of variables

-----
Current implementation for constructing parameter distributions for parameters read from file `uq_parameters.toml`: 
```
import CLIMAParameters
const CP = CLIMAParameters
param_set = CP.parse_toml_file("uq_test_parameters.toml")

# get `ParameterDistribution` for the parameter named "uq_param_1" (a 10d parameter of type `Parameterized`)
pd1 = CP.get_parameter_distribution(param_set, "uq_param_1")
# get `ParameterDistribution` for the parameter named "uq_param_5" (a 4-dim parameter of type `Samples`)
pd5 = CP.get_parameter_distribution(param_set, "uq_param_5")
# get `ParameterDistribution`s for parameters "uq_param_1" and "uq_param_5
pd = CP.get_parameter_distribution(param_set, ["uq_param_1", "uq_param_5"])
title: [WIP] Read and write UQ parameters
